rulings.Case Title: THE GOSEY REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED JANUARY 3, 2007
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Response To Supplemental Discovery Requests
rulings.Rulings: The Motion to Compel is granted, insofar as there is any information, produced in response to previously-propounded discovery, to update or correct. Request for sanctions is denied. There is no showing that there is any supplemental information to provide, and a phone call or other communication to establish this fact may have obviated the need for this motion. Persons appearing on their own behalf cannot recover attorney fees as sanctions. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, March 5, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LELAND FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Petitioner Barbara Platt's Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied. Although there is no factual dispute as to the material issues that attorney Ellis Ross Anderson is a named beneficiary of the Leland Family Trust as of the 2013 amendment, that he drafted the relevant 2013 trust amendment, and that the language of the Certificate of Independent Review does not match exactly the form provided in Probate Code § 21384, there does exist a material factual dispute over whether James Barringer, the attorney who signed the Certificate of Independent review, was "an independent attorney" for the decedent who adequately counseled her on the "nature and consequences" of her bequest to Mr. Anderson (quoted language from both current Probate Code § 21384(a) and former Probate Code § 21350(b)). As explained in Estate of Winans (2010) 183 Cal.App.4th 102, 115, the independent attorney's duty is to "make sure the transferor knows exactly what he or she is doing in executing the instrument." Former Probate Code § 21351 was in effect between January 1, 2003 and December 31, 2013. Current Probate Code § 21384 became effective January 1, 2011. Section 21384 restated the substance of § 21351 with three exceptions not relevant to this Motion, and added this sentence to the form language: "I am an "independent attorney" as defined in Section 21370 of the Probate Code and am in a position to advise the transferor independently, impartially, and confidentially as to the consequences of the transfer." There is no factual dispute that the Certificate of Independent Review Mr. Barringer signed in 2013 did not include this sentence. There is, however, a material disputed factual issue as to whether the Certificate of Independent Review that Mr. Barringer signed using the form language of former Probate Code § 21351was sufficiently "in substantially the following form" as the language specified in current Probate Code § 21384. The Court also acknowledges there is a related legal dispute presented in the papers over whether either former Probate Code § 21351 or current § 21384 governed the Certificate of Independent Review Mr. Barringer signed in 2013. However, because a motion for summary adjudication is not the proper means by which to adjudicate this legal dispute, the Court will not rule on that legal issue here. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) and opposing parties no later than 4:00 p.m. Tuesday, March 5, 2019.
